races.name: Centaurs
races.summary: Centaurs are presented as a consolidated race with several body-plan variants. The book treats Horse, Lion, and Lizard Centaurs separately for physical traits, temperament, and common culture choices.
races.notes:
- Centaurs share Centaur-speech as a racial language.
- Lion Centaurs can make unarmed attacks as Medium Claw attacks.
- Centaurs may not choose the Extra Limbs talent in the same way as two-legged races.
- The source gives each Centaur variant five background options.

races.name: Common Men
races.summary: Common Men are the baseline mortal human race, adaptable to almost any culture, profession, or circumstance.
races.languages:
Starting Languages: High-speech (S8/W6), Common-speech (S8/W6), Grey-Elvish (S6/W6), High-Elvish (S2/W2).

Allowed Adolescent Development: High-speech (S10/W10), Common-speech (S10/W10), Grey-Elvish (S8/W8), High-Elvish (S6/W6), Hill-speech (S6/W6), Sea-speech (S8/W8), Small-speech (S6/W6), Plains-speech (S6/W6).

races.name: Droloi
races.summary: Droloi are an unsettling Shadow World race with a scattered presence and no major population center. Their four eyes, pale leathery skin, claws, and hairless bodies make them appear alien to many other peoples.
races.languages:
Starting Languages: Elvish (S10/W10), Grey-Elvish (S8/W6), Common-speech (S8/W6), High-Elvish (S4/W4).

Allowed Adolescent Development: Grey-Elvish (S10/W10), Common-speech (S10/W10), High-Elvish (S10/W10), High-speech (S4/W4), Plains-speech (S8/W8), Wood-speech (S8/W8).
races.notes:
- Strong dark vision, including excellent sight in non-magical darkness.
- Leathery skin provides natural armor.
- Claws allow Medium Claw unarmed attacks.
- Notable tolerance for cold attacks, though not against below-freezing extremes.
- The source frames them as especially workable Fighters because of Strength and Constitution.

races.name: Dwarves
races.summary: Dwarves are rugged, practical, clannish, stubborn, and famed as miners, smiths, and artifact makers. The Shadow World equivalent is the Nomari, who share the same broad identity but have distinct setting-specific religious and physiological notes.
races.languages:
Extra Languages: High-speech (S5/W5), South-speech (S4/W4), North-speech (S5/W5).

Nomari extra languages: Loari (S8/W6), Dyari (S8/W6), Murlogi (S6/W6).
races.notes:
- Strong endurance and physical toughness.
- Heat and cold resistance for standard Dwarves.
- Shadow World Nomari have altered cold notes and a hibernation-related exception in the source.
- Strong fit for Fighter professions.
- Less natural fit for agile, socially focused, Essence, or Mentalism paths.
- Everyman skills include craft, mining, caving, and underground survival themes.

races.name: Elves
races.summary: Elves are immortal, graceful, perceptive, and magically resonant. The source consolidates RMFRP Wood, Grey, and High Elves with several Shadow World Elven peoples: Dyari, Erlini, Linaeri, Loari, and Shuluri.
races.notes:
- Elves are resistant to disease.
- Wood Elves have cold resistance and strong night vision short of absolute darkness.
- Elves have strong hearing; Wood Elves gain additional hearing-linked and performance-adjacent benefits in the source.
- Attribute bonuses make many Elves broadly capable, though the source notes some profession tension around Self-Discipline.
- Wood Elves are especially suitable as Bards, Dabblers, or stealth-capable characters.

races.name: Fey
races.summary: Fey are immortal, often reclusive peoples bound to wild or hidden places. This consolidated source entry includes small underground tricksters, woodland revelers, miniature riders, and Shadow World forest immortals.
races.notes:
- Fey are immortal but can die through violence or mischance.
- Dwelfs, Satyrs, and Tylweth Teg lean toward stealth, trickery, music, and agility-based roles.
- Tylweth Teg have a strong fear of iron and avoid iron or steel equipment.
- Lennai have major mystical affinities, forest ties, and vulnerability around iron.
- Several Fey variants receive discounted access to luck-related or lesser mystical/mental talents in the source.

races.name: Garks
races.summary: Garks are short, simian humanoids with prehensile tails. They live in elevated nests, move well through trees or cliffs, and are often feared as raiders, hunters, or mercenary fighters.
races.languages:
Starting Languages: Common-speech (S4/W4), plus one of Hill-speech (S8/W4), North-speech (S8/W4), or Wood-speech (S8/W4).

Allowed Adolescent Development: Common-speech (S8/W6), Hill-speech (S10/W8), North-speech (S10/W8), Wood-speech (S10/W8).

Shadow World starting languages: Gark (S8/W4), native regional language (S8/W4).

Shadow World allowed adolescent development: Gark (S10/W8), native regional language (S10/W8).
races.notes:
- The source frames Garks as physically durable and useful as Fighters, scouts, spies, or Rogues.
- They may not become non-Channeling spell users.
- Their background options emphasize plunder, toughness, and mercenary usefulness.

races.name: Gnomes
races.summary: Gnomes are long-lived, solitary, lore-loving mortals who resemble small, wizened humans. They are hospitable and curious, but reluctant to leave their homes, treasures, books, and carefully guarded lairs.
races.languages:
Starting Languages: Gnomish (S8/W8), optional Common-speech (S6/W6).

Allowed Adolescent Development: Gnomish (S10/W10), Common-speech (S8/W8), High-Elvish (S6/W6), High-speech (S8/W8).
races.notes:
- Poor natural fit for Fighters due to low Strength.
- Broad modest bonuses make them capable in several knowledge or spell-adjacent roles.
- The source highlights Clerics, Animists, Paladins, and Magents as possible fits.
- Gnomes are skilled linguists and may take rare books as wealth.

races.name: Goblinoids
races.summary: Goblinoids are a family of underground peoples. The source groups Goblins, Hobgoblins, Kobolds, and Murlogi together, but each has its own racial information table and now has its own page.

races.name: Goblins
races.summary: Goblins are crude, clever underground folk with a strong mechanical streak. They are nocturnal, live in lairs and warrens, and suffer badly in daylight.
races.languages:
Starting Languages: Goblin (S8/W6), optional Common-speech (S6/W4).

Allowed Adolescent Development: Common-speech (S8/W6), Goblin (S10/W8), Orcish (S4/W4).
races.notes:
- Dark vision in starlight, moonlight, and limited non-magical darkness.
- Sunlight vulnerability: -75 to all activities in daylight.
- Everyman skills: Mining, Metal-craft.

races.name: Gratar
races.summary: Import queued. Variants listed by the source include Black, Green, Grey, and Yellow Gratar.

races.name: Half-Elves
races.summary: Half-Elves are people of mixed Elven and Mannish ancestry. In the Shadow World, Half-Elven identity can also describe the Ta-Lairi, five related peoples: Eritari, Kytaari, Punkari, Sulini, and Vorloi.
races.languages:
- **Starting Languages:** Grey-elvish (S8/W6), Common-speech (S8/W6), High-elvish (S6/W5).
- **Allowed Adolescent Development:** Grey-elvish (S10/W10), Common-speech (S10/W10), High-elvish (S10/W10), High-speech (S10/W10), Elvish (S10/W10).
races.notes: The character creator currently reads the core Half-Elves row for race selection. The Ta-Lairi variants are preserved here for reference and future subrace support.

races.name: Half-Orcs
races.summary: Import queued.

races.name: Halflings
races.summary: Halflings are small, nimble folk with strong resilience, quickness, and cultural ties to small-speech communities.
races.languages:
Starting Languages: Small-speech (S8/W6), Common-speech (S8/W6).

Allowed Adolescent Development: Small-speech (S10/W10), Common-speech (S10/W10), High-speech (S8/W8), Grey-Elvish (S8/W8).

races.name: High Men
races.summary: High Men are taller, longer-lived, and more aristocratic than Common Men. They are associated with leadership, empire-building, and elevated social status.
races.languages:
Starting Languages: High-speech (S8/W6), Common-speech (S8/W6), Grey-Elvish (S6/W6), High-Elvish (S2/W2).

Allowed Adolescent Development: High-speech (S10/W10), Common-speech (S10/W10), Grey-Elvish (S8/W8), High-Elvish (S6/W6), Hill-speech (S6/W6), Sea-speech (S8/W8), Small-speech (S6/W6), Plains-speech (S6/W6).

races.name: Hirarazhir-Hirazi
races.summary: Import queued. The source treats Hirarazhir and Hirazi together.

races.name: Hobgoblins
races.summary: Hobgoblins are larger, stronger Goblinoids who often dominate Goblin groups. They tolerate daylight better than Goblins, though direct sun still hinders them.
races.languages:
Starting Languages: Goblin (S8/W6), optional Common-speech (S6/W4).

Allowed Adolescent Development: Common-speech (S8/W6), Goblin (S10/W8), Orcish (S4/W4).
races.notes:
- Dark vision in starlight, moonlight, and limited non-magical darkness.
- Sunlight vulnerability: -25 in direct sunlight.
- Everyman skills: Mining, Metal-craft.

races.name: Idivya
races.summary: Import queued.

races.name: Jhordi
races.summary: Import queued.

races.name: Kobolds
races.summary: Kobolds are smaller and weaker Goblinoids, more cunning than strong. The source notes that they are not highly recommended as player characters, but they are useful as a point of reference within the Goblinoid family.
races.languages:
Starting Languages: Goblin (S8/W6), optional Common-speech (S6/W4).

Allowed Adolescent Development: Common-speech (S8/W6), Goblin (S10/W8), Orcish (S4/W4).
races.notes:
- Dark vision in starlight, moonlight, and limited non-magical darkness.
- Sunlight vulnerability: -75 to all activities in daylight.
- Everyman skills: Mining, Metal-craft.

races.name: Laan
races.summary: Laan are a prominent mortal Shadow World race known as warriors, leaders, and empire-builders. In Jaiman they are known as the Zori.
races.languages:
Starting Languages: Laan (S8/W4), native regional language (S8/W4).

Allowed Adolescent Development: Laan (S10/W8), Emer or another appropriate native regional language (S10/W8), racial tongue of any mortal race (S4/W4).

races.name: Men
races.summary: The source folds Common Men, High Men, Mixed Men, Laan, and Umli into the broad Men entry. Each has its own racial information table and now has its own page.

races.name: Mermen
races.summary: Import queued.

races.name: Mixed Men
races.summary: Mixed Men descend from unions between Common Men and High Men. They sit between the two in build, lifespan, social position, and racial modifiers.
races.languages:
Starting Languages: High-speech (S6/W6), Common-speech (S8/W6).

Allowed Adolescent Development: High-speech (S8/W8), Common-speech (S10/W10), Grey-Elvish (S4/W4), High-Elvish (S2/W2), Hill-speech (S6/W6), Sea-speech (S6/W6), Small-speech (S6/W6), Plains-speech (S6/W6).

races.name: Murlogi
races.summary: Murlogi are the Shadow World equivalent of Goblins. They live in subterranean settlements and are often encountered as mercenaries or operators of siege engines and machines of war.
races.languages:
Starting Languages: Murlogi (S8/W4), native regional language (S8/W4).

Allowed Adolescent Development: Murlogi (S10/W8), native regional language (S10/W6), Lugroki (S2/W2).
races.notes:
- Dark vision in starlight, moonlight, and limited non-magical darkness.
- Sunlight vulnerability: -40 in daylight.
- Everyman skills: Mining, Metal-craft.

races.name: Orcs
races.summary: Import queued. The source consolidates Common Orcs, Greater Orcs, Lesser Lugroki, and Greater Lugroki.

races.name: Quishadi
races.summary: Import queued.

races.name: Shuikmar Sharkmen
races.summary: Import queued.

races.name: Sohleugir-Saurkur
races.summary: Import queued. The source treats Sohleugir, Lizardmen, and Saurkur together.

races.name: Troglodytes-Trogli
races.summary: Import queued. The source treats Troglodytes and Shadow World Trogli as equivalent for this entry.

races.name: Umli
races.summary: Umli are hardy mortal people of the frozen North of Kulthea. They are thickly built, cold-adapted, and shaped by the memory of displacement from more fertile lands.
races.languages:
Starting Languages: Umli (S8/W4), native regional language (S8/W4).

Allowed Adolescent Development: Umlii (S10/W8), native regional language (S10/W8).
races.notes: Umli have strong cold-survival themes in the source and may take Geographic Awareness and Unnatural Stamina at reduced background-option costs.

races.name: Urlocs
races.summary: Import queued.

races.name: Vulfen Wolfmen
races.summary: Vulfen, also called Wolfmen, are lupine humanoids suited to mobile, predatory, and borderland roles.
races.languages:
Starting Languages: Common-speech (S8/W6), plus either Plains-speech (S6/W6) or Wood-speech (S6/W6), depending on native environment.

Allowed Adolescent Development: Common-speech (S10/W8), either North-speech (S6/W6) or South-speech (S6/W6), and either Plains-speech (S8/W8) or Wood-speech (S8/W8), depending on native environment.

professions.name: Alchemist
professions.summary: Alchemists are pure Essence spell users skilled at relating magic to the physical universe. They manipulate matter, shape items, and imbed spells from all three realms into physical objects, storing power for later use.

professions.name: Animist
professions.summary: Animists are pure Channeling spell users whose magic is bound to the living world. Their base lists focus on movement through natural terrain, plant and animal influence, herb lore, protection from the wild, and reading the signs of nature.

professions.name: Armsmaster
professions.summary: Armsmasters are semi spell users of Mentalism. They serve as household knights, mercenary commanders, military officers, or elite specialists. Their magic improves personal martial ability, survival, military organization, and battlefield command.

professions.name: Astrologer
professions.summary: Astrologers are hybrid spell users who combine Channeling and Mentalism. Their magic gathers information by observing celestial bodies and supports detection, communion, precognition, and communication. Many abilities are fully usable only under starlight.

professions.name: Bard
professions.summary: Bards combine Mentalism with arms training. Their magic focuses on sound, lore, performance, and the use of items.

professions.name: Cleric
professions.summary: Clerics are pure Channeling spell users whose power is received through a deity. Their access to powerful magic is balanced by religious obligations.

professions.name: Dabbler
professions.summary: The Dabbler is a semi-spell user combining Essence with arms training. Their magic focuses on stealth, detection, perception, movement, locks, and traps.

professions.name: Enchanter
professions.summary: Enchanters are hybrid spell users who combine Essence and Mentalism. Their magic misdirects and manipulates the minds of humanoids, beasts, animals, and other sentient creatures through mental illusions, mind control, and memory alteration.

professions.name: Fighter
professions.summary: Fighters are non-spell users trained to develop weapon skills and wear heavy armor efficiently. They are less suited to maneuvering, mechanical tasks, and magic.

professions.name: Healer
professions.summary: Healers are pure Channeling spell users devoted to restoring damaged bodies and cleansing afflictions. Their base lists divide healing into blood, muscle, bone, organs, surface injuries, and purification.

professions.name: Illusionist
professions.summary: Illusionists are pure spell users who specialize in illusion and misdirection. Their base magic manipulates sensory experience, and they have advantages with perception, stalking, and hiding.

professions.name: Lay Healer
professions.summary: Lay Healers are pure Mentalism spell users who repair bodies through focused, intimate restorative discipline. Their base lists address blood, muscle, bone, concussion trauma, nerves, organs, and prosthetic restoration.

professions.name: Magent
professions.summary: Magents are semi spell users who combine the realm of Mentalism with the realm of Arms. Their base lists support information gathering, subterfuge, and stealthy missions.

professions.name: Magician
professions.summary: Magicians are pure Essence spell users whose base lists focus on elemental manipulation.

professions.name: Mana Molder
professions.summary: Mana Molders are pure spell users of Essence who concentrate on Mana Forms: solid manifestations of Essence. Their spells create temporary items, protective fields, and mana creatures ranging from simple servants to warriors capable of following basic orders.

professions.name: Mentalist
professions.summary: Mentalists are pure Mentalism spell users who specialize in the interaction of minds: perception, communication, control, attack, and mental merging.

professions.name: Monk
professions.summary: Monks are semi spell users who combine the realm of [[Essence]] with martial training. Their base lists focus on personal enhancement, unusual movement, heightened senses, recovery, and disciplined control of the body.

professions.name: Mystic
professions.summary: Mystics are hybrid spell users who combine Essence and Mentalism. Their magic emphasizes subtle misdirection, personal illusion, and the alteration of matter.

professions.name: Mythic
professions.summary: Mythics are semi spell users who combine Arms with [[Channeling]]. They often stand outside normal church hierarchies and undertake secret missions for their religion and deity.

professions.name: Priest
professions.summary: Priests are configurable pure [[Channeling]] spell users designed for a specific religion. The companion provides a common template, then modifies it according to the deity's sphere of influence. This allows priesthoods from different faiths to have distinct skills, bonuses, spell lists, and special abilities.
everymanSkills: Determined by the deity's sphere of influence
occupationalSkills: Determined by the deity's sphere of influence
restrictedSkills: Determined by the deity's sphere of influence

professions.name: Ranger
professions.summary: Rangers combine Channeling with arms training. Their base magic supports wilderness travel, weather, concealment, and survival.

professions.name: Rogue
professions.summary: Rogues are flexible non-spell users who balance arms training with thief-like skills. Their costs sit between the Fighter and Thief specializations.
everymanSkills: choice of one Situational Awareness skill

professions.name: Runemage
professions.summary: Runemages are hybrid spell users of Essence and Channeling who concentrate on the power written and spoken language has over reality. Most are Nomenist casters. They use words, symbols, and runes to create enchantments such as wards while using the Primal Tongue to manipulate the physical world and its creatures.

professions.name: Seer
professions.summary: Seers are pure Mentalism spell users who specialize in gathering information. Their magic evokes the past, scries the present, divines the future, and modifies the senses of themselves and others.

professions.name: Sorcerer
professions.summary: Sorcerers are hybrid spell users who combine Essence and Channeling. Their base magic focuses on the destruction of animate and inanimate material.

professions.name: Summoner
professions.summary: Summoners are hybrid spell users who combine [[Channeling]] and [[Essence]]. Their magic communicates with, summons, controls, and enhances creatures ranging from animals to extraplanar beings and distant allies.

professions.name: Thief
professions.summary: Thieves are non-spell users who excel at mechanical manipulation, maneuvering, and stealth. They also develop weapon skills at a reasonable cost.
everymanSkills: Choice of one Situational Awareness skill

professions.name: Warlock
professions.summary: Warlocks are hybrid spell users who combine [[Channeling]] and [[Mentalism]]. They manipulate people and fate through harmful magic, broken faith, fear, revenge, transformation, and ill fortune.

professions.name: Warrior Mage
professions.summary: Warrior Mages are semi spell users who combine the realm of Arms with [[Essence]]. Their base spells adapt Essence casting to combat and survival in hostile environments. They are elite fighters specialized in attacking enemy spell casters on their own terms.

spellLists.name: Arcane Barriers
spellLists.summary: Open Arcane barrier magic for creating walls of light, air, water, wood, earth, ice, stone, ground material, and force.

spells.name: Lightwall
spells.description: Creates an insubstantial black wall that blocks light and vision but not movement.

spells.name: Airwall
spells.description: Creates dense, churning air that halves movement through it and penalizes attacks through it.

spells.name: Lightwall II
spells.description: As Lightwall, but with longer duration and larger area.

spells.name: Waterwall
spells.description: Creates a wall of water that greatly slows movement and attacks through it.

spells.name: Woodwall
spells.description: Creates a wooden wall that can be burned, chopped, or toppled.

spells.name: Earthwall
spells.description: As Woodwall, but made of packed earth.

spells.name: Icewall I
spells.description: As Woodwall, but made of ice.

spells.name: Airwall True
spells.description: As Airwall, but with longer duration.

spells.name: Barrier Pit
spells.description: Opens a pit in earth, ice, or stone within range.

spells.name: Stonewall
spells.description: As Woodwall, but made of stone.

spells.name: Waterwall True
spells.description: As Waterwall, but with longer duration.

spells.name: Woodwall True
spells.description: As Woodwall, but permanent.

spells.name: Earthwall True
spells.description: As Earthwall, but permanent.

spells.name: Icewall II
spells.description: As Icewall I, but with increased area.

spells.name: Stonewall True
spells.description: As Stonewall, but permanent.

spells.name: Meld Walls
spells.description: Fuses two touching walls of the same material or fuses stone blocks.

spells.name: Groundwall
spells.description: Forms a wall from the predominant ground material nearby.

spells.name: Curved Wall
spells.description: Curves a lower-level wall spell up to a semicircle.

spells.name: Blend Walls
spells.description: As Meld Walls, but the walls may be dissimilar materials.

spells.name: Construction
spells.description: As Stonewall True, but forms a freestanding structure up to the listed surface area.

spells.name: Groundwall True
spells.description: As Groundwall, but permanent.

spells.name: Wall of Force
spells.description: Creates a transparent wall of force that is impassable.

spells.name: Wall of Force True
spells.description: As Wall of Force, but with the listed duration/range.

spellLists.name: Arcane Forces
spellLists.summary: Open Arcane force and movement magic for telekinesis, flight, gravity manipulation, aiming, hurling, and magnetism.

spells.name: Staying I
spells.description: Exerts one pound of one-direction pressure on a person or object.

spells.name: Telekinesis I
spells.description: Moves one object up to 1 pound slowly; if concentration stops, it remains as if stayed.

spells.name: Levitation I
spells.description: Moves the target vertically at 10' per round.

spells.name: Staying III
spells.description: As Staying I, but with a 25-pound pressure limit.

spells.name: Flying I
spells.description: Target flies at 100' per round.

spells.name: Telekinesis III
spells.description: As Telekinesis I, but with a 25-pound limit.

spells.name: Levitation X
spells.description: As Levitation I, but at 100' per round.

spells.name: Flying III
spells.description: As Flying I, but at 300' per round.

spells.name: Aiming
spells.description: Adds +50 to a missile attack while the caster concentrates on the firer and missile.

spells.name: Reduce Gravity
spells.description: Halves gravity in the area, reducing falling damage and penalizing movement.

spells.name: Hurling I
spells.description: Hurls a small object as a Sling attack using directed spell skill.

spells.name: Flying True
spells.description: As Flying I, but speed is based on caster level.

spells.name: Telekinesis True
spells.description: As Telekinesis I, but with a much larger weight limit.

spells.name: Staying True
spells.description: As Staying I, but with a caster-level pressure limit.

spells.name: Increase Gravity
spells.description: Triples gravity in the area, penalizing movement and attacks and increasing exhaustion.

spells.name: Mass Aiming
spells.description: As Aiming, but affects multiple archers and missiles.

spells.name: Hurling IV
spells.description: As Hurling I, but with greater weight and impact.

spells.name: Rapid Gravity Shifts
spells.description: Throws the target randomly and inflicts repeated impact criticals.

spells.name: Mass Flying
spells.description: As Flying I, but affects multiple targets.

spells.name: Magnetize
spells.description: Surrounds the target with an intense magnetic field that attracts ferrous material.

spells.name: Aiming True
spells.description: As Aiming, but the attack automatically hits and may resolve as a slaying attack.

spells.name: Critical Mass
spells.description: Causes the target object to attract nearby matter violently.

spellLists.name: Arcane Senses
spellLists.summary: Open Arcane sensory enhancement magic for hearing, sight, smell, touch, vibration sense, breathing adaptation, illusion piercing, resilience, and sensory merging.

spells.name: Sly Ears
spells.description: Doubles normal hearing and grants bonuses to hearing-based Awareness.

spells.name: Nightvision
spells.description: Allows clear-night sight as if day, requiring some residual light.

spells.name: Quiet Step
spells.description: Mufflles the target's step and improves Stalking.

spells.name: Scent
spells.description: Grants acute smell and bonuses to smell-based Awareness.

spells.name: Watervision
spells.description: As Nightvision, but underwater or in other liquids.

spells.name: Touch
spells.description: Grants tactile sensitivity useful for touch-based actions and Awareness.

spells.name: Sharp Eyes
spells.description: Grants extremely accurate vision and vision-based Awareness bonuses.

spells.name: Darkvision
spells.description: As Nightvision, but without light and through magical darkness.

spells.name: Scent True
spells.description: As Scent, but detects extremely faint or old scents.

spells.name: Lightvision I
spells.description: Allows sight in any brightness of light, but not total darkness.

spells.name: Vibration Sense
spells.description: Detects vibrations through solid material and aids Awareness.

spells.name: Changing Lungs
spells.description: Allows breathing air, water, or gas at will.

spells.name: Nightvision True
spells.description: As Nightvision, but at normal daylight sight range.

spells.name: Disillusion True
spells.description: Causes illusions within range to cease existing for the target.

spells.name: Skin Resilience
spells.description: Gives the target skin resilience comparable to minor drake armor.

spells.name: Stop Poison
spells.description: Suspends existing poisons in the target for the duration.

spells.name: Sensory True
spells.description: Combines Sly Ears, Scent, Touch, and Sharp Eyes.

spells.name: Sensory Merge
spells.description: Merges the caster's senses with a subject at a known exact location.

spells.name: Arcane Sense
spells.description: Allows one lower-level spell from this list to be used each round.

spellLists.name: Arcane Shield
spellLists.summary: Open Arcane defensive magic for force shielding, magnetic repulsion, missile and melee deflection, spell deflection, re-aiming attacks, and temporary arcane armor.

spells.name: Shielding
spells.description: Creates an invisible force shield that functions like a normal shield.

spells.name: Repel
spells.description: Repels ferrous objects, penalizing certain missiles, melee attacks, and metal-using maneuvers.

spells.name: Shield
spells.description: As Shielding, but with duration.

spells.name: Enchanted Defense
spells.description: Temporarily enchants clothing, armor, or shield for a DB bonus.

spells.name: Deflections I
spells.description: Deflects one visible missile within range.

spells.name: Bladeturn I
spells.description: Deflects one visible melee attack within range.

spells.name: Greater Deflections
spells.description: Allows one missile deflection per round with concentration.

spells.name: Greater Bladeturn

spells.name: Spell Deflect I

spells.name: Aim Untrue II

spells.name: Mass Deflections

spells.name: Re-Aiming

spells.name: Spell Deflect II

spells.name: Mass Bladeturn

spells.name: Mass Aim Untrue
spells.description: As Aim Untrue I, but up to caster level in missiles.

spells.name: Arcane Armor

spells.name: Greater Spell Deflect

spells.name: Re-Aiming True

spells.name: Deflect True

spells.name: Arcane Armor True

spellLists.name: Arcane Signs
spellLists.summary: Open Arcane symbol magic for analyzing, bypassing, placing, and removing magical signs, including signs that impose hesitation, confusion, shock, fear, sleep, blindness, paralysis, or death.

spells.name: Analyze Sign
spells.description: Reveals a sign's trigger and stored effect.

spells.name: Unsign I
spells.description: Removes a Sign I effect after a resistance contest.

spells.name: Bypass Sign
spells.description: Prevents a target sign from triggering for the duration.

spells.name: Sign I
spells.description: Places a first-level spell into a sign on an inanimate object.

spells.name: Sign of Hesitation
spells.description: Places a sign that limits target activity after a failed RR.

spells.name: Sign III
spells.description: As Sign I, but stores up to a third-level spell.

spells.name: Sign of Confusion
spells.description: As Sign of Hesitation, but imposes action penalties.

spells.name: Unsign III
spells.description: Removes signs up to Sign III and certain lower special signs.

spells.name: Sign of Shock
spells.description: Places a sign that shocks the target on a failed RR.

spells.name: Sign V
spells.description: As Sign I, but stores up to a fifth-level spell.

spells.name: Sign of Fear
spells.description: Places a sign that makes the target flee after a failed RR.

spells.name: Unsign V
spells.description: Removes signs up to Sign V and certain special signs.

spells.name: Sign of Sleep
spells.description: Places a sign that causes magical sleep after a failed RR.

spells.name: Sign VIII
spells.description: As Sign I, but stores up to an eighth-level spell.

spells.name: Sign of Blinding
spells.description: Places a sign that blinds the target after a failed RR.

spells.name: Unsign VIII
spells.description: Removes signs up to Sign VIII and certain special signs.

spells.name: Sign of Paralysis
spells.description: Places a sign that paralyzes the target after a failed RR.

spells.name: Sign X
spells.description: As Sign I, but stores up to a tenth-level spell.

spells.name: Mass Sign
spells.description: As a Sign spell, but affects up to caster level in targets.

spells.name: Unsign True
spells.description: Removes any lower-level sign spell.

spells.name: Sign of Death
spells.description: Places a sign that kills the target after a failed RR.

spells.name: Sign True
spells.description: As Sign I, but stores a spell of any level.

spells.name: Mass Unsign
spells.description: As Unsign True, but removes multiple signs.

spellLists.name: Detection Law
spellLists.summary: Open Arcane detection and analysis magic for magic, life, invisible things, traps, illusions, power, locations, future intuition, and broad omniscient sensing.

spells.name: Lesser Analysis
spells.description: Analyzes a recent non-organic item for age, purpose, and material.

spells.name: Detect Magic
spells.description: Detects active spells or items and reports realm.

spells.name: Presence
spells.description: Senses sentient or thinking beings in the area.

spells.name: Detect Life
spells.description: As Detect Magic, but detects living beings.

spells.name: Detect Invisible
spells.description: As Detect Magic, but detects invisible things and reduces attack penalties against them.

spells.name: Detect Traps
spells.description: Detects traps with a base chance modified by trap complexity.

spells.name: Death's Tale
spells.description: Shows events surrounding the death of a nearby corpse and the killer if present.

spells.name: Detect Illusion
spells.description: Checks one object or place for illusion.

spells.name: Perceive Power I
spells.description: As Detect Magic, but estimates power level.

spells.name: Power Typing
spells.description: Analyzes spell type and list; on a person, gives exact profession.

spells.name: Location
spells.description: Gives direction and distance to a familiar or well-described object or place.

spells.name: Intuitions
spells.description: Gives a vision of likely events in the next ten minutes if a specified event occurs.

spells.name: Greater Analysis
spells.description: As Lesser Analysis, but no age limit and works on organic objects and people.

spells.name: Perceive Power III
spells.description: As Perceive Power I, but with greater range.

spells.name: Detect Spell
spells.description: Detects spells previously cast in the area within the listed time window.

spells.name: Greater Location
spells.description: As Location, but with greater range.

spells.name: Detect True
spells.description: As any one lower Detect spell, but with greater range.

spells.name: Perceive Power True
spells.description: As Perceive Power I, but with greater range.

spells.name: Life Analysis
spells.description: Gives detailed analysis of a living target.

spells.name: Intuitions True
spells.description: As Intuitions, but can look farther into the future by caster level.

spells.name: Location True
spells.description: As Location, but with greater range.

spells.name: Omniscience
spells.description: Uses lower-level spells from this list simultaneously, except instant-duration spells.

spellLists.name: Power Manipulation
spellLists.summary: Open Arcane power-source magic for sensing sources, assessing their nature, storing power, tapping sources, channeling spells to other spell users, and exchanging power points.

spells.name: Sense Power I
spells.description: Senses sources of power within range and gives direction and distance.

spells.name: Assess Source
spells.description: Reveals the nature, amount, and species divisions of a detected source.

spells.name: Channels I
spells.description: Channels the caster's next first-level spell to another spell user.

spells.name: Power Storage I
spells.description: Temporarily stores one PP per caster level in an item for later tapping.

spells.name: Tap Source I
spells.description: Links the caster to a source and replenishes PPs at 1 PP per minute.

spells.name: Channels III
spells.description: As Channels I, but transmits up to third-level spells.

spells.name: Sense Power II
spells.description: As Sense Power I, but with greater area.

spells.name: Power Storage II
spells.description: As Power Storage I, but stores 2 PPs per level.

spells.name: Power Merge
spells.description: Allows two spell casters to interchange PPs.

spells.name: Channels V
spells.description: As Channels I, but transmits up to fifth-level spells.

spells.name: Tap Source III
spells.description: As Tap Source I, but at 3 PPs per minute.

spells.name: Power Storage III
spells.description: As Power Storage I, but stores 3 PPs per level.

spells.name: Sense Power V
spells.description: As Sense Power I, but with greater area.

spells.name: Power Storage IV
spells.description: As Power Storage I, but stores 4 PPs per level.

spells.name: Channels X
spells.description: As Channels I, but transmits up to tenth-level spells.

spells.name: Tap Source V
spells.description: As Tap Source I, but at 5 PPs per minute.

spells.name: Lord Channels
spells.description: As Channels I, but transmits up to twentieth-level spells.

spells.name: Tap Source True
spells.description: As Tap Source I, but at 1 PP per round.

spellLists.name: Realm Law
spellLists.summary: Open Arcane meta-magic for detecting spell realms, resisting or canceling spells, studying and simulating realm spells, dispelling, creating spell walls, blocking channels, masking spell signatures, and altering resistance.

spells.name: Detect Magic I
spells.description: Senses active spells and areas of effect without revealing details.

spells.name: Cancel Spell
spells.description: Forces an attacking spell to resist before the caster must resist it.

spells.name: Detect Magic II
spells.description: As Detect Magic I, but reveals realm, type, and caster profession.

spells.name: Detect Residual Magic
spells.description: Reveals realm and type of previously cast spells in the area.

spells.name: Study Spell
spells.description: Studies an active spell for later use with Three Realms spells.

spells.name: Detect Magic III
spells.description: As Detect Magic I, but gives PP cost and exact spell name.

spells.name: Three Realms III
spells.description: Simulates a previously studied Open or Closed spell up to third level.

spells.name: Dispel
spells.description: Attempts to dispel an active spell.

spells.name: Spell Wall
spells.description: Creates a wall incorporating an eligible spell of no greater than eighth level.

spells.name: Elemental Wall
spells.description: Creates a wall that inflicts an elemental critical when passed through.

spells.name: Block Channel
spells.description: Prevents a target from sending or receiving channeled spells or PPs unless resisted.

spells.name: Unpower
spells.description: Removes the target's remaining PPs and prevents spell casting for the duration.

spells.name: Spell Masking I
spells.description: Makes the caster's spells appear as a chosen type for detections and divinations.

spells.name: Altered Resistance
spells.description: Gives targets of the caster's spells a resistance penalty.

spells.name: Three Realms VI
spells.description: As Three Realms III, but up to sixth-level spells.

spells.name: Spell Masking II
spells.description: As Spell Masking I, but can choose realm and caster profession.

spells.name: Three Realms X
spells.description: As Three Realms III, but up to tenth-level spells.

spells.name: Spell Masking True
spells.description: As Spell Masking II, but may appear as any spell the caster knows.

spells.name: Realm Master
spells.description: Allows one lower-level spell from this list to be used each round.

spellLists.name: Spell Inhibitors
spellLists.summary: Open Arcane suppression magic for sensing inhibition zones and creating zones that prevent spells by list, realm, or all realms up to specified levels.

spells.name: Sense Inhibition
spells.description: Senses direction and distance to inhibition zones in the area.

spells.name: Spell Inhibition II
spells.description: Prevents spells up to second level from a specific spell list.

spells.name: Realm Inhibition II
spells.description: As Spell Inhibition II, but affects a whole realm.

spells.name: Total Inhibition II
spells.description: As Spell Inhibition II, but affects spells from any realm.

spells.name: Spell Inhibition IV
spells.description: As Spell Inhibition II, but prevents spells up to fourth level.

spells.name: Realm Inhibition IV
spells.description: As Spell Inhibition IV, but affects a whole realm.

spells.name: Total Inhibition IV
spells.description: As Spell Inhibition IV, but affects spells from any realm.

spells.name: Spell Inhibition VI
spells.description: As Spell Inhibition II, but prevents spells up to sixth level.

spells.name: Realm Inhibition VI
spells.description: As Spell Inhibition VI, but affects a whole realm.

spells.name: Total Inhibition VI
spells.description: As Spell Inhibition VI, but affects spells from any realm.

spells.name: Spell Inhibition True
spells.description: As Spell Inhibition II, but prevents spells up to tenth level.

spells.name: Realm Inhibition True
spells.description: As Spell Inhibition True, but affects a whole realm.

spells.name: Total Inhibition True
spells.description: As Spell Inhibition True, but affects spells from any realm.

spellLists.name: Spell Triggers
spellLists.summary: Open Arcane spell-storage trigger magic for detecting, analyzing, storing, sensing, delaying, bypassing, and enhancing trigger conditions.

spells.name: Detect Trigger
spells.description: Detects triggers within the area, concentrating on small areas each round.

spells.name: Analyse Trigger
spells.description: Reveals which spell is in a known trigger and what will trigger it.

spells.name: Trigger V
spells.description: Stores the caster's next spell in the target, up to the listed level limit.

spells.name: Sense Trigger I
spells.description: Gives a stored spell a sensory trigger condition.

spells.name: Time Delay I

spells.name: Enhanced Sense Trigger I
spells.description: As Sense Trigger I, but with a larger Alertness bonus for the triggering condition.

spells.name: Trigger X
spells.description: As Trigger V, but stores higher-level spells.

spells.name: Bypass Trigger

spells.name: Sense Trigger II
spells.description: As Sense Trigger I, but with two triggering senses or conditions.

spells.name: Time Delay II
spells.description: As Time Delay I, but with longer duration.

spells.name: Trigger XV
spells.description: As Trigger V, but stores still higher-level spells.

spells.name: Enhanced Sense Trigger II
spells.description: As Sense Trigger II, but with a larger Alertness bonus.

spells.name: Sense Trigger True
spells.description: As Sense Trigger I, but allows up to five trigger conditions.

spells.name: Time Delay True
spells.description: As Time Delay I, but with much longer duration.

spells.name: Enhanced Sense Trigger True
spells.description: As Sense Trigger True, but with a larger Alertness bonus.

spells.name: Lord Trigger
spells.description: As Trigger V, but stores spells up to twentieth level or target level, whichever is lower.

spells.name: Trigger True
spells.description: As Lord Trigger, but target level does not limit the stored spell.

spellLists.name: Holy Mission
spellLists.summary: Divination, guidance, and infiltration spells that help the Mythic carry out covert religious missions. Provides awareness, subtle communication, and faith-directed perception.

spells.name: Urban Lore I
spells.description: +10 bonus to next maneuver using an Urban skill.

spells.name: Question I
spells.description: Target must answer one single-concept question they can understand.

spells.name: Holy Vision
spells.description: Caster receives a vague dream about a mission from their deity or church.

spells.name: Guess
spells.description: When facing a choice with little information, GM biases the result 25% in caster's favour.

spells.name: Silent Spell
spells.description: Caster's next spell is cast silently — no sound is made despite normal casting.

spells.name: Cloud Perception
spells.description: Target becomes preoccupied; -20 to -40 penalty to Awareness group skills.

spells.name: Intuitions I
spells.description: Vision of what will probably happen in the next minute if caster takes a specific action.

spells.name: Faith's Wisdom
spells.description: +30 bonus to all Religion skill maneuvers.

spells.name: Urban Lore III
spells.description: As Urban Lore I, except bonus is +30.

spells.name: Stunning
spells.description: Caster channels raw divine power at target; target is stunned.

spells.name: Question X
spells.description: As Question I, except target must answer up to 10 single-concept questions.

spells.name: Death's Tale
spells.description: Vision of events surrounding the death of a nearby corpse, including the killer. Corpse within 10', died within caster's level in years.

spells.name: Channel Opening
spells.description: Caster makes contact with a known acquaintance and learns their direction and distance.

spells.name: Intuitions V
spells.description: As Intuitions I, except caster may see 5 minutes into the future.

spells.name: Holy Vision True
spells.description: As Holy Vision, except the caster is shown mission details and where to begin.

spells.name: Blinding
spells.description: As Stunning, except target is blinded for the duration.

spells.name: Commune I
spells.description: Receives a yes/no answer from patron deity on a single topic. Maximum once per day.

spells.name: Misperception
spells.description: Target misperceives events — sees nothing or something vague; cannot make obvious violence seem normal.

spells.name: Absolution
spells.description: [RR Mod: -20] Target's soul is torn from body; target is unconscious and all healing suffers -75. Soul returns only via Lifegiving.

spells.name: Death's Tale True
spells.description: As Death's Tale but corpse may be any age; caster learns reason of death, exact killer, and who was ultimately responsible.

spells.name: Commune True
spells.description: As Commune, but caster can receive a more detailed answer (name or short phrase) on one concept.

spells.name: Absolution Pure
spells.description: As Absolution, except soul can only return via Lifegiving.

spells.name: High Prayer
spells.description: Caster receives detailed information from deity on a single topic question.

spellLists.name: The Defender
spellLists.summary: Protection and defensive magic for the Mythic. Provides elemental resistance, force shields, armour conjuration, deflection of weapons, and combat defence bonuses.

spells.name: Protection I
spells.description: -5 to elemental attacks vs caster; +5 to all RRs vs spells. Doubles to +10 vs same-faith Channeling casters.

spells.name: Fear Defense
spells.description: +15 bonus to fear RRs.

spells.name: Poison Defense
spells.description: +15 bonus to poison RRs.

spells.name: Shield
spells.description: Invisible force shield in front of caster; functions as a normal shield (-25 to appropriate attacks) without occupying a hand. Cannot combine with a real shield.

spells.name: Protection II
spells.description: As Protection I, except bonuses are +10 and +15.

spells.name: Combat Defense I
spells.description: +10 DB vs melee and missile attacks. Not cumulative with Aura or Blur spells.

spells.name: Deflections I
spells.description: Caster can deflect any one visible missile within 10'; subtracts 100 from missile's attack.

spells.name: Mythical Armor I
spells.description: Caster's clothing is enchanted to protect as AT 3.

spells.name: Bladeturn I
spells.description: Caster can deflect any one visible melee attack within range; subtracts 50 from the attack.

spells.name: Protection III
spells.description: As Protection I, except bonuses are +15 and +20.

spells.name: Conversion Defense
spells.description: +30 vs attempts to convert caster; same bonus to RRs vs spells from other religions seeking conversion or corruption.

spells.name: Mythical Armor II
spells.description: Caster's clothing is enchanted to protect as AT 8.

spells.name: Deflections II
spells.description: As Deflections I, except affects two missiles.

spells.name: Combat Defense II
spells.description: As Combat Defense I, except bonus is +15.

spells.name: Protection IV
spells.description: As Protection I, except bonuses are +20 and +30.

spells.name: Bladeturn II
spells.description: As Bladeturn I, except affects two attacks.

spells.name: Mythical Armor III
spells.description: Caster's clothing is enchanted to protect as AT 12.

spells.name: Soul Defense
spells.description: +20 vs Absolution spells and Black Channel spells. Cumulative with other protection spells.

spells.name: Combat Defense III
spells.description: As Combat Defense I, except bonus is +20.

spells.name: Mythical Armor IV
spells.description: Caster's clothing is enchanted to protect as AT 20.

spells.name: Protection True
spells.description: As Protection I, except bonuses are +30 and +50.

spells.name: Combat Defense True
spells.description: As Combat Defense I, except bonus is +30.

spells.name: The Defender
spells.description: Caster may use any lower-level spell on this list once per round.

spellLists.name: The Eye
spellLists.summary: Detection and tracking magic for the Mythic. Enables sensing of holy/unholy forces, enemies, magic, traps, invisible beings, and marked targets at range.

spells.name: Detect Holy/Unholy
spells.description: Senses presence of Holy/Unholy objects, consecrated ground, or Channeling works. Caster may concentrate on a different area each round.

spells.name: Detect Enemies I
spells.description: Detects total number of enemies in area with approximate direction and distance; reveals most heavily represented enemy type. Will reveal if caster's Mark is present.

spells.name: Mark
spells.description: Caster places a mental mark on one target, enabling use of spells on The Eye and Vengeance lists vs that target. Max one Mark per three levels (round up).

spells.name: Detect Magic
spells.description: Detects any active spell or item from any realm. Caster learns realm and type of spells in the area.

spells.name: Detect Traps
spells.description: 75% chance of detecting a trap in the area; trap sophistication may modify the chance.

spells.name: Tracking
spells.description: All Tracking maneuvers receive a +50 bonus.

spells.name: Detect Invisible
spells.description: Detects all invisible objects or beings in area. Attacks against detected targets are modified by -50.

spells.name: Detect Illusion
spells.description: Caster can check one object or place (up to 10'R) and determine if it is or bears an illusion.

spells.name: Perceive Power
spells.description: As Detect Magic, and also gives an estimate of the power level of person, item, or spell examined.

spells.name: Detect Enemies II
spells.description: As Detect Enemies I, except for range and area of effect.

spells.name: Detect Poison
spells.description: Detects any poisons on a single object or being.

spells.name: Detect Holy/Unholy True
spells.description: As Detect Holy/Unholy, except for range and duration.

spells.name: Direction
spells.description: Caster learns direction and distance to any one of his Marks.

spells.name: Detect Status
spells.description: Attempts to learn the target's Divine Status level from their mind.

spells.name: Memory's Path
spells.description: Caster can remember an exact route travelled, even if senses were impaired; must be cast within one month of the journey. Duration equals caster's level in hours.

spells.name: The Eye
spells.description: Caster receives a divine vision of one of his Marks; reveals only general activities (sleeping, fighting, etc.); Mark must be within range.

spells.name: Tracking True
spells.description: As Tracking, except bonus is +75.

spells.name: Perceive Power True
spells.description: As Perceive Power, except for range and area of effect.

spells.name: Location
spells.description: Gives direction and distance to a specific object or place caster is familiar with, provided caster knows its dimensions within 10% and at least two unique attributes.

spells.name: Detect Enemies True
spells.description: As Detect Enemies I, except for range and area of effect.

spells.name: Divine Sight
spells.description: Caster sees through illusions, facades, invisibility, and other misrepresentations of the truth.

spells.name: Divine Eye
spells.description: As The Eye, except caster does not need to know exactly where the target is located.

spells.name: True Eye
spells.description: Caster may use any one lower-level spell on this list once per round.

spellLists.name: The Mask
spellLists.summary: Deception, disguise, and concealment magic for the Mythic. Enables the caster to change appearance, conceal aura and alignment, mislead pursuers, and take on other identities.

spells.name: Conceal Aura
spells.description: Hides caster's Channeling aura from Detect Holy/Unholy and similar spells.

spells.name: Alter Feature I
spells.description: Changes a single minor facial feature (hair colour, eye colour, small scar, etc.).

spells.name: False Scent
spells.description: Caster has a different scent; animals and Tracking rolls that rely on scent are confused or misled.

spells.name: Alter Voice
spells.description: Caster can mimic any voice heard for at least 1 round; +40 to impersonation attempts.

spells.name: Conceal Alignment
spells.description: Caster's divine alignment registers as Neutral to detection spells.

spells.name: Alter Feature II
spells.description: As Alter Feature I, except multiple features may be changed.

spells.name: Mislead
spells.description: Leaves a false trail, misdirecting pursuers; Tracking rolls against caster suffer -50 for duration.

spells.name: Disguise
spells.description: Changes caster's apparent race, sex, and general body shape; does not alter height or mass by more than 10%.

spells.name: False Thoughts
spells.description: Caster's surface thoughts appear mundane and harmless to telepathic probing; +40 to RR vs mind-reading.

spells.name: Conceal Aura True
spells.description: As Conceal Aura, except also masks any unique divine signature even to True Detection.

spells.name: Alter Feature True
spells.description: As Alter Feature II, except changes are permanent until dispelled.

spells.name: False Identity
spells.description: Complete appearance change; caster perfectly mimics a specific person seen for at least 1 hour; +60 to impersonation.

spells.name: Shadow Walk
spells.description: Caster moves without casting a shadow and makes no sound; -40 to perception rolls to detect movement.

spells.name: Mislead True
spells.description: As Mislead, except Tracking rolls suffer -75 and false trail is much more convincing.

spells.name: Conceal History
spells.description: Caster's past actions are concealed from Commune, Detect Status, and divine inquiry spells.

spells.name: Vanish
spells.description: Caster becomes invisible; invisibility ends if caster attacks or casts a spell.

spells.name: False Identity True
spells.description: As False Identity, but also copies voice, scent, and mannerisms; +80 to impersonation.

spells.name: Mind Blank
spells.description: Caster's mind is completely hidden; immune to telepathy, scrying, and Commune spells targeting caster.

spells.name: False Aura
spells.description: Caster can assume any divine alignment for detection purposes; doubles conceal bonuses from lower spells on this list.

spells.name: The Mask
spells.description: Caster becomes a perfect double of a target they have observed for at least 1 day; all attributes, voice, scent, and mannerisms are replicated.

spells.name: Vanish True
spells.description: As Vanish, but invisibility is maintained even while attacking; does not end unless caster chooses or spell expires.

spells.name: Perfect Mask
spells.description: As The Mask, except perfect replication; even divine detection requires an Absurd maneuver to pierce the disguise.

spellLists.name: The Will
spellLists.summary: Endurance, self-healing, and mental fortitude magic for the Mythic. Enables the caster to resist fatigue, recover from wounds, ignore pain, and push the body beyond normal limits through divine will.

spells.name: Resist Pain I
spells.description: Caster ignores up to -5 penalty from wounds/pain.

spells.name: Endurance I
spells.description: Caster does not tire; Exhaustion Points are not lost for 1 hour.

spells.name: Steady
spells.description: Caster stabilises at 0 hits; will not lose additional hits from a bleeding wound for the duration.

spells.name: Calm
spells.description: +20 bonus to all RRs vs fear and emotional manipulation.

spells.name: Resist Pain II
spells.description: As Resist Pain I, except penalty ignored is up to -15.

spells.name: Minor Recovery
spells.description: Caster recovers 5 concussion hits instantly.

spells.name: Endurance II
spells.description: As Endurance I, except duration is 1 day/level; Exhaustion Points lost at half normal rate.

spells.name: Focus
spells.description: +25 to all RRs; caster must concentrate each round (C).

spells.name: Resist Pain True
spells.description: As Resist Pain I, except all wound/pain penalties are ignored.

spells.name: Moderate Recovery
spells.description: Caster recovers 10 concussion hits instantly.

spells.name: Calm True
spells.description: As Calm, except bonus is +40 and covers all mental attack RRs.

spells.name: Iron Mind
spells.description: Immune to all compulsion, charm, and domination effects.

spells.name: Endurance True
spells.description: As Endurance II, except Exhaustion Points are not lost at all; caster does not need sleep.

spells.name: Major Recovery
spells.description: Caster recovers 20 concussion hits instantly.

spells.name: Fortitude
spells.description: Caster's body hits are treated as double for purposes of critical threshold; does not actually increase hits.

spells.name: Resist Stun
spells.description: Caster is not stunned by concussion hits below 50% of max hits in a single round.

spells.name: Regenerate I
spells.description: Caster regenerates 2 hits per round.

spells.name: Iron Will
spells.description: +50 to all RRs; all stun effects are halved (round down).

spells.name: True Recovery
spells.description: Caster fully recovers all concussion hits instantly.

spells.name: Regenerate II
spells.description: As Regenerate I, except caster regenerates 5 hits per round.

spells.name: The Will
spells.description: Caster ignores all wound and pain penalties; immune to stun, fear, charm, and compulsion.

spells.name: Regenerate True
spells.description: Caster regenerates 10 hits per round and removes one negative status per round (bleeding, stunned, etc.).

spells.name: Undying
spells.description: Caster cannot be reduced below 1 hit; while active, caster cannot die from concussion.

spellLists.name: Vengeance
spellLists.summary: Offensive combat magic for the Mythic, focused on striking marked targets, disrupting enemy spellcasters, and delivering divine punishment. Most powerful spells require a Mark (from The Eye list) on the target.

spells.name: Strike I
spells.description: Delivers a Holy Channeling attack: 1d100 + 20 vs target's AT.

spells.name: Weaken I
spells.description: Target suffers -10 to all attacks and RRs.

spells.name: Disrupt Spell I
spells.description: Interrupts a spell being cast; caster's target loses their action this round and must re-cast next round.

spells.name: Holy Wound I
spells.description: Delivers a Holy Channeling critical strike (A severity) to a Marked target; bypasses normal AT.

spells.name: Strike II
spells.description: As Strike I, except attack is 1d100 + 40.

spells.name: Weaken II
spells.description: As Weaken I, except penalty is -20.

spells.name: Blind
spells.description: Marked target is blinded; RR Mod -10.

spells.name: Disrupt Spell II
spells.description: As Disrupt Spell I, except target loses the spell and PP used.

spells.name: Strike True
spells.description: As Strike I, except attack is 1d100 + 60 and delivers a Holy critical.

spells.name: Holy Wound II
spells.description: As Holy Wound I, except critical severity is B.

spells.name: Cripple
spells.description: Marked target's movement is reduced to 25%; RR Mod -20.

spells.name: Weaken True
spells.description: As Weaken I, except penalty is -30 to all actions.

spells.name: Disrupt Spell True
spells.description: As Disrupt Spell II, except target is stunned for 1 round per 10 failure.

spells.name: Holy Wound III
spells.description: As Holy Wound I, except critical severity is C.

spells.name: Divine Strike
spells.description: As Strike True, except attack is 1d100 + 80; ignores up to 10 points of AT protection.

spells.name: Seize
spells.description: Marked target is held motionless; RR Mod -20; target may attempt RR each round.

spells.name: Holy Wound IV
spells.description: As Holy Wound I, except critical severity is D.

spells.name: Wither
spells.description: Marked target loses half their remaining hits over 1 day; RR Mod -30.

spells.name: Smite
spells.description: As Divine Strike, except delivers a Holy critical at D severity automatically in addition to the attack.

spells.name: Holy Wound True
spells.description: As Holy Wound I, except critical severity is E; no RR allowed.

spells.name: Vengeance
spells.description: Full divine punishment: 1d100 + 100 Holy attack, plus E-severity critical; bypasses all AT and 50% of magic protection.

spells.name: Divine Wrath
spells.description: As Vengeance, except target is knocked back 50' and stunned for 1 round per 10 failure even if RR succeeds.

spells.name: Judgment
spells.description: Target's soul is judged; on failure, target is destroyed (no resurrection); RR Mod -50. Irreversible.

spellLists.name: Concealment Mastery
spellLists.summary: Dabbler spells for hiding the caster or objects through shadows, illusions, displacement, darkness, and invisibility.

spells.name: Shadow
spells.description: The caster and carried objects appear shadowed, making them almost invisible in darkness and often granting a substantial bonus to Stalking/Hiding maneuvers.

spells.name: Unseen III
spells.description: Makes up to three objects invisible until the duration ends, the object is struck violently, or it makes a violent move such as an attack.

spells.name: Conceal I
spells.description: Gives one inanimate object the visual appearance of a similar object, within normal size limits; this is an illusion.

spells.name: Locate Object
spells.description: Finds an inanimate object if the caster can uniquely describe it.

spells.name: Darkness
spells.description: Creates darkness around the touched point, moving with the touched object or being if applicable.

spells.name: Secret I
spells.description: Gives one inanimate object both the visual and tactile appearance of another object, within normal size limits; this is an illusion.

spells.name: Unseen V
spells.description: As Unseen III, but affects up to five objects.

spells.name: Displacement I
spells.description: Offsets the caster's apparent position; attacks against the caster have an initial 10% automatic miss chance that drops by 5% after each miss.

spells.name: Conceal III
spells.description: As Conceal I, but affects up to three objects.

spells.name: Unseen X
spells.description: As Unseen III, but affects up to ten objects.

spellLists.name: Influences
spellLists.summary: Dabbler spells for sleep, charm, suggestion, telepathy, and temporarily holding a humanoid target.

spells.name: Sleep III
spells.description: Sends targets into natural sleep, affecting up to three total levels; the first two rounds are magical sleep and cannot be broken by normal waking methods.

spells.name: Charm Animals
spells.description: Target animals believe the caster is a good friend.

spells.name: Charm Kind
spells.description: A target humanoid believes the caster is a good friend.

spells.name: Sleep V
spells.description: As Sleep III, but affects up to five total levels.

spells.name: Suggestion
spells.description: The target follows one suggested act, provided the act is not completely alien or self-destructive.

spells.name: Telepathy I
spells.description: Lets the caster read surface thoughts; if the target's resistance roll succeeds by more than 25, the target realizes someone tried to read their thoughts.

spells.name: Sleep VII
spells.description: As Sleep III, but affects up to seven total levels.

spells.name: Hold Kind
spells.description: Holds a target humanoid to 25% activity.

spells.name: Greater Charm Kind
spells.description: As Charm Kind, but affects one target per five caster levels, or fraction thereof.

spells.name: Sleep X
spells.description: As Sleep III, but affects up to ten total levels.

spellLists.name: Mechanisms
spellLists.summary: Dabbler spells for understanding, tuning, jamming, unlocking, operating, and disarming mechanical devices.

spells.name: Machine Lore
spells.description: Analyzes one machine, revealing its purpose and operation; grants the caster a special bonus to operate it and a smaller bonus to those they instruct.

spells.name: Tune
spells.description: Restores a machine's efficiency to its original level, such as returning a rusty lock to normal operation.

spells.name: Jam I
spells.description: Has a 50% chance to jam the target mechanism; if jammed, it has only a 5% chance to operate each time it is used or triggered.

spells.name: Unlock I
spells.description: Has a 30% chance to open a nonmagical lock; a failed attempt has a chance to set off associated traps.

spells.name: Untrap I
spells.description: As Unlock I, but for disarming traps; a failed attempt has a chance to trigger the trap.

spells.name: Operate I
spells.description: As Unlock I, but operates a nonmagical machine in its normal capacity for the spell duration.

spells.name: Detect Active Power
spells.description: Alerts the caster when active magic is within the area, though not its nature, realm, or exact source.

spells.name: Jam II
spells.description: As Jam I, but with a 70% chance to jam and only a 4% chance to set off the mechanism.

spells.name: Unlock II
spells.description: As Unlock I, but with a 50% chance to open the lock and an 8% chance to set off associated traps.

spells.name: Untrap II
spells.description: As Untrap I, but with a 50% chance to disarm the trap and an 8% chance to trigger it.

spellLists.name: Movement Mastery
spellLists.summary: Dabbler spells for running, jumping, landing safely, silent movement, swimming, balance, climbing surfaces, diving, and leaving no tracks.

spells.name: Run
spells.description: Lets the caster move at twice normal walking pace while expending exhaustion as if walking; the spell ends when the caster stops or takes another action.

spells.name: Longjump
spells.description: Lets the caster jump much farther horizontally or vertically and safely land from jumps within the spell's limits.

spells.name: Landing
spells.description: Lets the caster land safely from a fall up to 20' per caster level, reducing longer falls by that amount.

spells.name: Shadow Steps
spells.description: Silences the caster's movement while they move no faster than walking pace.

spells.name: Underwater Movement
spells.description: Allows the caster to move underwater as if on land.

spells.name: Balance I
spells.description: Grants +50 to balance-based movement maneuvers performed at walking pace.

spells.name: Spider's Step
spells.description: Allows half-walking movement along solid surfaces angled up to 90 degrees, while maintaining three-point contact.

spells.name: Long Dive
spells.description: Lets the caster dive safely through the air up to 50' per level if sufficient water is present.

spells.name: Traceless Passing
spells.description: Allows walking-pace movement without leaving tracks or other visible signs of passage.

spells.name: Spider's Step True
spells.description: As Spider's Step, but allows movement along any surface, including ceilings.

spellLists.name: Senses
spellLists.summary: Dabbler spells that enhance hearing, sight, smell, touch, peripheral vision, darkvision, watervision, and remote sensing.

spells.name: Sly Ears
spells.description: Doubles normal hearing and grants bonuses to awareness skills involving hearing.

spells.name: Nightvision
spells.description: Lets the caster see 100' on a normal night as if it were daylight.

spells.name: Scent
spells.description: As Sly Ears, but for smell, granting an extremely acute sense of smell and related awareness bonuses.

spells.name: Sensory Marker
spells.description: Marks one location so the caster can later locate it with a Far Sense spell; the caster can maintain up to one marker per level.

spells.name: Far Sense I
spells.description: Lets the caster use one sense from a marked location within range; the remote point can be rotated, but the caster must remain immobile and concentrate.

spells.name: Sidevision
spells.description: Gives the caster a 300 degree field of vision and reduces flank and rear attack bonuses against them.

spells.name: Touch
spells.description: Grants extreme tactile sensitivity and a bonus to actions depending on touch, such as picking locks or disarming traps.

spells.name: Watervision
spells.description: Lets the caster see 100' through water, including murky water, as if it were daylight.

spells.name: Darkvision
spells.description: As Nightvision, but the caster can see through any darkness and needs no light.

spells.name: Far Sense II
spells.description: As Far Sense I, but the caster may use up to two senses at the marked location.

spellLists.name: Thieving Law
spellLists.summary: Dabbler spells for assessing wealth, marking and locating targets, finding buyers, valuing objects, and identifying magical properties.

spells.name: Gauge Wealth
spells.description: Gives the caster a sense of the target's total wealth or net worth, which may include non-coin assets.

spells.name: Weigh Pockets
spells.description: Tells the caster approximately how much wealth the target carries on their person.

spells.name: Mark Target
spells.description: Magically marks one target for later use with Locate Target; only one target can be marked at a time.

spells.name: Find Buyer I
spells.description: Locates the nearest person in the area who wants a specific object; the caster must touch the object while casting.

spells.name: Locate Target
spells.description: Reveals the exact direction and distance to a marked target.

spells.name: Jewel/Metal Assessment
spells.description: Assesses the value of jewels and metals within 10%, including different cultural values known to the caster; it does not assess craftsmanship or magic.

spells.name: Item Assessment
spells.description: As Jewel/Metal Assessment, but also accounts for the item's craftsmanship; magical properties are not evaluated.

spells.name: Find Buyer II
spells.description: As Find Buyer I, but locates someone who both wants the object and is willing to acquire it now.

spells.name: Detect Power
spells.description: Detects whether an item has magical power, but not the realm or amount of power.

spells.name: Item Analysis I
spells.description: Gives the caster a 10% chance to identify an enchanted object's abilities, rolled separately for each ability; the same item cannot be checked again until the caster gains a level.
